reasoning: The stock closed at 130.65 on August 29, establishing the threshold for all future resolution dates. The fundamental headwind in the short term is an ex-dividend date on September 2 for $0.52, which mechanically lowers the stock's opening price. To overcome this and reach the 130.65 threshold by September 7, the stock must gain about 0.4% from its ex-div baseline. Over a 5-day trading window, this hurdle puts the probability of a YES resolution below 50% (estimated at 0.42, supported by the YFinance trend forecast of 0.41). As the horizon extends to September 30 (1 month), the variance grows, meaning the initial $0.52 drag matters less, pulling the probability closer to 0.5 (estimated 0.46). By November 29 (3 months), standard volatility further dwarfs the initial dividend drag, so the probability approaches a coin flip with a very slight upward equity drift (0.49). Finally, by February 2026 (6 months), the forecast relies heavily on the long-term upward drift of the broader market, balanced by instructions to keep distant forecasts close to 0.5, yielding an estimate of 0.51.
